description: Proteins that catalyze biochemical reactions; deficiency typically causes metabolic disorders.
Proteins that slow down cell division or repair DNA errors; loss of function leads to cancer.
Provides physical support or shape to cells and tissues (e.g., muscle, bone, blood).
Controls the movement of ions or molecules across cell membranes.
Proteins that bind to DNA to turn other genes on or off.
Genes that normally help cells grow; mutations can permanently activate them, causing cancer.
Specialized proteins that identify and correct damage to the DNA sequence.
Proteins specifically involved in the blood clotting cascade.
Proteins that control the immune system's ability to recognize and fight pathogens.
Ligands or receptors involved in transmitting information between or within cells.
